meta_title: For Founders: analytics setup to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Analytics Setup to Solve Project Abandonment
content.seo_description: Stop project abandonment. Use this expert checklist to implement analytics using Antigravity and Claude Code to validate features and finish your SaaS MVP.
content.answer_block: Project abandonment stems from a lack of feedback loops. By implementing event-based analytics, you shift from 'guessing' to 'measuring' user behavior. This creates a data-driven path to completion, ensuring every line of code you write directly correlates to a validated business outcome, keeping your momentum high and focus sharp.
content.intro:
The graveyard of SaaS is littered with 'almost finished' projects—brilliant ideas that died because the founder lost the signal in the noise. You feel the stagnation: a feature creep paralysis that turns your excitement into a chore. Without objective data, you are flying blind, building for ghosts rather than paying customers.

But the remedy isn't more willpower; it's radical clarity. By integrating a lean analytics stack early, you stop building in a vacuum. You begin to see where users actually click, where they drop off, and most importantly, what features actually move the needle. It’s time to move from the 'dreaming' phase to the 'shipping' phase.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metric
steps.description: Before writing code, identify the one action that proves your user finds value. Analytics is useless without a target.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Identify the primary conversion event for a B2B SaaS dashboard and suggest a schema for tracking user onboarding completion.
steps.claude_code_command: claude create --file analytics_schema.md --prompt 'Define a minimal event schema for user signups and feature activation.'

steps.title: Automated Event Instrumentation
steps.description: Inject tracking events into your core user flows without bloating your codebase manually.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a wrapper function for PostHog or Mixpanel events that handles user identity and common properties automatically.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --file analytics_wrapper.ts --prompt 'Implement a singleton analytics service that captures button clicks and page views.'

steps.title: Set Up Real-Time Monitoring
steps.description: Connect your events to a dashboard so you can see user activity as it happens, reinforcing the feeling of progress.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure a dashboard layout for tracking daily active users and feature-specific funnel drop-offs.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Setup dashboard configuration for top 3 funnel steps.'

steps.title: Implement Session Recording
steps.description: Watch how users interact with your MVP to identify friction points that cause abandonment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide instructions for integrating session replay tools into a Next.js application.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --package @posthog/posthog-js --prompt 'Initialize session recording in the main layout file.'

steps.title: Validate Features via A/B Testing
steps.description: Stop arguing with yourself about design. Let users vote with their clicks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a feature flag implementation pattern to test two variations of a pricing page CTA.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create a feature flag toggle for the new hero section variation.'

pro_tips: Track only what you will act on; vanity metrics kill focus faster than no metrics at all.
Use session recording on your landing page to see exactly where users get confused before they sign up.
Set up automated Slack alerts for your North Star metric—the dopamine hit of a real user action keeps you building.
Don't build your own analytics backend; use established SaaS providers to save 40+ hours of engineering time.
Correlate your 'can't finish' feeling with low usage data—often, you stop building because you haven't validated the feature's necessity.
content.next_steps: Deploy your analytics-enabled MVP to a small group of beta testers, monitor the funnel, and iterate based on the first 50 user sessions.
content.conversion_block: Stop guessing and start shipping. By aligning your development with real user data, you eliminate the uncertainty that causes project abandonment. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
